scripts.archive:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
cd "$(dirname "$0")"
cargo clean 2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf target/debug target/release 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f *.log 2>/dev/null || true

focus: Memory safety: no unwrap() on network/IO results — use proper error handling
MQTT message handling: verify topic patterns and QoS levels
Govee LAN API protocol compliance — check against docs/ reference
Concurrency: no data races, proper use of Arc/Mutex where shared state exists
Error propagation: use Result<T, E> chains, avoid silent failures
Test coverage for new device types or protocol changes
ignore: Do not suggest async runtime changes (tokio vs async-std) without benchmarks
Do not flag cargo fmt issues — CI catches those

descriptionMust: What changed: which modules, device support, or protocol handling
Why: what bug, device, or feature this addresses
Testing: cargo test output, and if applicable, tested with physical Govee hardware
rules: Keep it concise — no boilerplate sections if they'd be empty
Note if PR adds support for new Govee device models
Keep it short and descriptive — max 4 words after prefix

general.description: Rust bridge between Govee smart home devices and MQTT/Home Assistant. Supports LAN API discovery, BLE fallback, and HA add-on packaging.
rules: Prefer explicit error handling over expect()/unwrap()
Keep add-on config (addon/) in sync with any new CLI flags or env vars
Test with real Govee devices when possible — note in PR if hardware-tested
Solo developer — no team process overhead needed

outputFormatting.structure: Always use markdown: headers (##/###), bullets, bold for key terms, tables for comparisons
outputFormatting.tldr: Lead every response with a single TL;DR line before any detail
outputFormatting.decisions: For multi-option decisions: Option A / B / C with explicit tradeoffs in a table or bullet list — never buried in prose
outputFormatting.density: Max 3 sentences per paragraph. Break up walls of text. Prefer visual hierarchy over long paragraphs.
outputFormatting.codeBlocks: Always fence code with language hint (```rust, ```toml, ```bash). Never inline code longer than one expression.
outputFormatting.citations: When referencing files, use relative paths as inline code (`src/foo.rs`). When referencing external docs, link them.
